evals.name: launch-connect-arm64-perf-session
evals.prompt: i'm about to start a 2-3 hr session debugging a memory issue on a rust binary that needs to actually run on arm64 (we ship to graviton). need a fresh dev box — give me something beefy enough to compile + run perf, i'd say c7g.2xlarge — and drop me right into a shell. tell me how to detach and reattach because i'll need to step away partway through to take a call.
evals.expected_output: A single `kleya launch` command using c7g.2xlarge (or larger Graviton) with --connect, an explanation that they land in tmux with detach via Ctrl-b d, and the reattach command `kleya connect <name>` using the name kleya prints at launch. Should not invent flags. Should not fall back to raw `aws ec2 run-instances`.

evals.name: two-named-templates-in-config
evals.prompt: i keep switching between launching a tiny scratch box for editing and a bigger one for compiling. want to set up two templates so i can just do `kleya launch --template scratch` or `kleya launch --template compile`. scratch should be t4g.medium, compile should be c7g.2xlarge. both on spot, default region eu-west-1. show me the config.toml i need.
evals.expected_output: A valid TOML config at ~/.config/kleya/config.toml with default_region = "eu-west-1", a [defaults] section setting market = "spot", and two [[templates]] blocks named "scratch" (t4g.medium) and "compile" (c7g.2xlarge). Should mention `kleya template create` as an alternative or note that the file location is `~/.config/kleya/config.toml`. Bonus: mention `kleya config show` to verify.

evals.name: list-and-selective-terminate
evals.prompt: ran `kleya list` and i've got like 5 instances. one's called kleya-fuzzy-otter that i remember was for the migration test, and a couple others i don't remember. how do i figure out what each is and kill the ones i'm not using anymore? i don't want to accidentally kill the migration one.
evals.expected_output: Should suggest `kleya list --json` to see tags/launch-time/instance-id, or connecting briefly to inspect. Should NOT autonomously run `kleya terminate --yes` against anything. Should call out that `--yes` is a footgun and the interactive confirm is the safety belt. Should explicitly preserve kleya-fuzzy-otter. May suggest adding [[templates.tags]] in future to make this easier.

evals.name: unattended-implementation-handoff
evals.prompt: we just finished planning the auth-rewrite refactor — plan's at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-18-auth-rewrite.md. when you're finished planning, spin up a remote instance and use it for unattended implementation. i want to grab dinner and come back in a couple hours to check progress.
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) `kleya launch --connect` (or launch then connect) to land on a fully-bootstrapped box; (2) get the plan/repo onto the remote (git push + git clone, or scp); (3) start `claude` inside a detached/named tmux window on the remote with the plan path, piping output to a log file (e.g. `tee impl.log`); (4) detach so work survives disconnect; (5) print the reattach command (`kleya connect <name>` + how to find the impl window) for the user to use later; (6) NOT autonomously terminate the box. Should flag --dangerously-skip-permissions as a real safety tradeoff and ideally confirm before using it. Should NOT just `nohup … &` — tmux is the right primitive.

evals.name: offload-long-test-suite
evals.prompt: i need to run our integration suite — it's like 45 minutes of mostly cpu-bound rust tests plus a handful that need ~16gb of memory. my laptop fan is dying and i want to take a walk. can you offload this to kleya? i'll need the junit xml and coverage html back when it's done so i can attach them to the PR.
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) pick a beefy Graviton instance type sized for the workload (c7g.4xlarge or similar — at least 8 vCPU and 16+ GiB RAM); (2) discuss spot vs on-demand briefly given the 45-minute runtime (spot is fine but eviction risk exists); (3) `kleya launch --connect`; (4) clone the repo on the remote (or rsync uncommitted changes, excluding target/); (5) run the suite in a named tmux window with output teed to a log file so it survives disconnect; (6) detach and give the user the reattach command; (7) explain how to pull artifacts back via scp/rsync before terminating (e.g. junit XML and coverage HTML); (8) suggest `kleya terminate` only after artifacts are retrieved, and mention saving a template for repeat runs. Should NOT autonomously terminate the box, and should NOT just background with `nohup … &`.
